title: N2 road to close for blasting
description: EASTERN CAPE NEWS - The South African...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"EASTERN CAPE NEWS - The South African National Roads Agency Limited (SANRAL) has advised road users that blasting is scheduled to take place on the N2 national road between KwaBhaca and EmaXesibeni in the Eastern Cape.","html":"EASTERN CAPE NEWS - The South African National Roads Agency Limited (SANRAL) has advised road users that blasting is scheduled to take place on the N2 national road between KwaBhaca and EmaXesibeni in the Eastern Cape."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The blasting will take place tomorrow, Friday, 13 June at 12:00, approximately 8.8 kilometres (km) from KwaBhaca when travelling towards EmaXesibeni."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The road will be closed during the blasting activities for a maximum of 30 minutes.","html":"The road will be closed during the blasting activities for a maximum of 30 minutes."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Motorists are asked to plan their trips accordingly, consider alternative routes and to use caution when making use of the road,” SANRAL said.","html":"“Motorists are asked to plan their trips accordingly, consider alternative routes and to use caution when making use of the road,” SANRAL said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Motorists travelling from Kokstad towards Mthatha may consider the following alternative routes:","html":"Motorists travelling from Kokstad towards Mthatha may consider the following alternative routes: "},{"type":"list","items":["Go onto the R56 route and travel past Matatiele and then past Tlokoeng until Nqanqarhu, then take the R396 towards Tsolo until you join the N2, and then proceed to Mthatha.","Stay on the N2 until Pakade (18km out of Kokstad), then turn left onto the R394 and join the R61 at Magusheni. Slip right onto the R61 towards Flagstaff, Lusikisiki and Port St Johns and then rejoin the N2 in Mthatha. Similarly, motorists travelling from Mthatha towards Kokstad may consider using the same routes.","The shortest alternative route is 69 kilometres longer and may add approximately 50 minutes to your trip between Mthatha and Kokstad."],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"SANRAL has apologised for any inconvenience caused. - SAnews.gov.za","html":"SANRAL has apologised for any inconvenience caused. - SAnews.gov.za"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: N2 road to close for blasting
